vBRIEFInfo.description: Story vBRIEF for #1643 (consumer discoverability for task packs:slice) plus the #1637 --list-packs pack-discovery affordance it depends on; delivered as one coherent story under epic #1284.

plan.title: Pack discovery (--list-packs) + consumer discoverability wiring for packs:slice

narratives.Description: Make the content-pack slice surface discoverable end-to-end so a consumer agent actually knows to use it. First add a pack-level discovery affordance (task packs:slice --list-packs) so the surface is self-describing: --list-packs to find packs, then <pack> --list to find slices. Then wire that discovery path into the surfaces a consumer agent loads -- the managed AGENTS.md section, main.md, and skill routing -- so the agent loads lessons via the slice instead of reading the whole 100KB file or never loading them at all. Every wiring surface references the discovery commands (--list-packs / --list), never hardcoded pack or slice names, so the v2 expansion (#1637) adds packs and slices without churning this wiring.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1. Add --list-packs to scripts/packs_slice.py: discover available packs by scanning the pack registry (e.g. packs/*/ sources or vbrief/schemas/*-pack.schema.json), print pack short-name + version + one-liner, same text|json + provenance + three-state-exit conventions as the existing --list; extend tests/cli/test_packs_slice.py in-process. 2. Add a Content Packs note to templates/agents-entry.md naming task deft:packs:slice --list-packs and task deft:packs:slice <pack> --list as the canonical discovery + lessons read-path; run task agents:refresh to regenerate AGENTS.md from the template (byte-identical refresh). 3. Upgrade main.md's Continuous Improvement lessons reference (lines ~241-245) from write-only (codify in lessons.md) to also name the slice read-path (load relevant lessons via task packs:slice before implementing). 4. Add a skill-routing trigger so lessons / prior-art queries route to the slice surface. 5. Extend tests/content/test_agents_entry_contract.py: add the new rule to the curated marker list (#1309) and assert the rendered section references --list-packs / --list and does NOT hardcode individual slice or pack names.
narratives.UserStory: As a consumer agent that just installed deft, I want the loaded guidance to name task packs:slice --list-packs as the way to discover and load lessons, so that I read only the slice I need instead of the whole lessons file or skipping lessons entirely.
narratives.Traces: planRef #1643; affordance Refs #1637; epic #1284; mirrors the #1274 discoverability fix; pilot #1294; design #1283.

narrative.Acceptance: task packs:slice --list-packs displays each available pack's short-name, version, and description read from the pack registry, emits text by default and json under --json, and a new pack appearing in the registry shows up without any code change.

items.title: Consumer managed-section names the discovery path

narrative.Acceptance: After task agents:refresh, the rendered AGENTS.md managed section names task deft:packs:slice --list-packs and <pack> --list as the lessons read-path, and the byte-identical-refresh contract test still passes.

items.title: main.md and skill routing name the slice read-path

narrative.Acceptance: main.md documents loading lessons via the slice alongside the existing write-path, and a skill-routing trigger directs lessons / prior-art queries to task packs:slice.

items.title: Wiring references discovery, never enumerates names

narrative.Acceptance: A contract test asserts the rendered managed section references --list-packs and --list and rejects any hardcoded individual slice or pack name, so v2 pack/slice additions require no edit here.

expected_outputs: rendered AGENTS.md names the discovery path without hardcoding slice/pack names

references.title: Issue #1643: consumer discoverability for task packs:slice
